assertions.expectation: Proposes a phased approach: control plane first, then CPU pools, then T4 dev, then inference (A100), then training (H100) last
assertions.reason: Response clearly outlines 4-week phased approach: Week 1 control plane + CPU services, Week 2 T4 development, Week 3 A100 inference, Week 4 H100 training

assertions.expectation: Control plane can be upgraded independently without affecting running workloads
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'Training jobs unaffected (control plane upgrade doesn't impact running pods)' and upgrades control plane first

assertions.expectation: CPU service pools upgraded first as lowest risk — use standard surge settings (maxSurge=2-3)
assertions.reason: CPU services pool upgraded in Phase 1 with maxSurge=5% (reasonable surge setting), labeled as 'Low Risk' for stateless services

assertions.expectation: T4 dev pool upgraded next — dev workloads are tolerant of interruption
assertions.reason: T4 development pool upgraded in Phase 2/Week 2, explicitly noting 'Dev workloads are restart-tolerant and lower priority'

assertions.expectation: A100 inference pool upgraded with careful surge settings (maxSurge=1) and PDBs to maintain serving capacity
assertions.reason: A100 inference uses maxSurge=0 (even more conservative than expected), includes PDB configuration with minAvailable=85%, and monitors inference capacity

assertions.expectation: H100 training pool upgraded last, during a scheduled gap between training runs — with cordon-and-wait or maxSurge=0/maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: H100 training upgraded in final Phase 4, uses maxSurge=0/maxUnavailable=2 with coordination for job transitions and maintenance exclusions during active campaigns

assertions.expectation: Notes the 20-node parallelism limit — at 5,000 nodes, the full upgrade will take weeks and must span multiple maintenance windows
assertions.reason: Response shows duration calculations (e.g., '~20 batch limit', '75 batches of 4 nodes') and explicitly states 4-week timeline with maintenance windows

assertions.expectation: Mentions GPU driver compatibility testing in staging before each GPU pool upgrade
assertions.reason: Includes validation checklist with 'GPU drivers compatible (T4 typically has good compatibility)' and risk mitigation section mentions 'Test 1.32 in staging with representative workloads'

assertions.expectation: Addresses GPUDirect/RDMA networking validation after upgrading the H100 pool
assertions.reason: Training-specific validation includes 'NCCL/RDMA connectivity preserved' and 'GPUDirect-TCPX functioning (GKE 1.32 supports this)' with risk mitigation for RDMA/Interconnect validation
